text: All we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
context: Gandalf comforting Frodo by emphasizing agency and responsibility over despair about fate.

text: Even the smallest person can change the course of the future.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (film)
context: Encouragement that courage and action matter more than size or apparent power.

text: A wizard is never late, nor is he early, Frodo Baggins. He arrives precisely when he means to.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
context: Playful reminder of purpose, timing, and not being bound by others' expectations.

text: The world is indeed full of peril, and in it there are many dark places; but still there is much that is fair, and though in all lands love is now mingled with grief, it grows perhaps the greater.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
context: Balancing acknowledgment of suffering with hope and the deepening of love through hardship.

text: Courage is found in unlikely places.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
context: Hope that bravery can emerge from the most humble or unexpected sources.

text: Many that live deserve death. And some that die deserve life. Can you give it to them? Then do not be too eager to deal out death in judgement.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
context: Warning against hasty judgment and the dangers of playing god with life and death.

text: I will not say: do not weep; for not all tears are an evil.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
context: Gentle permission to grieve, recognizing that sorrow can be part of healing and love.

text: There is only one Lord of the Ring, only one who can bend it to his will. And he does not share power.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
context: Caution about the corrupting nature of absolute power and the importance of resisting it.
